indicator_text: The register includes multiple lobbyist types (professional/consultant lobbyists; in-house company lobbyists; in-house organization/association lobbyists; law firms; public entities and government-employee lobbyists).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + registration form + faq. Score 1 if the state's registration requirements explicitly cover at least 3 of the following lobbyist types: (a) contract/consultant lobbyists, (b) in-house company lobbyists, (c) in-house association/nonprofit lobbyists, (d) law-firm lobbyists, (e) government-employee lobbyists. Score 0 if fewer than 3 types are covered.

indicator_text: There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + registration form. Score 1 if the state's registration threshold is at or below the federal LDA benchmark ($3,500 quarterly / ~$14,000 annually in lobbying income OR 20% of time spent lobbying). Score 0 if the threshold is higher than the federal benchmark (casts a narrower net). Record the actual threshold in notes.
notes: Federal LDA benchmark used for defensibility; alternative 'median across states' was considered but rejected as circular.

indicator_text: The register's targets-of-lobbying definition covers legislative branches, executive branch officials, senior agency staff/administrators, and legislative/executive staff. [US-state translation of FOCAL's Westminster terminology.]
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + registration form. US-state role translation: 'Ministers / Deputy Ministers' → governor + lt. governor + cabinet secretaries; 'members of parliament' → state legislators; 'Director-Generals and senior officials' → agency heads and deputy heads; 'staff' → legislative staff + gubernatorial staff. Score 1 if the state's definition covers at least 3 of: legislators, executive-branch officials (gov/cabinet), agency heads, staff. Score 0 if fewer than 3.
notes: Original FOCAL text uses Westminster vocabulary; this operationalization maps to US-state equivalents. Original verbatim text preserved in focal_2024_indicators.csv.

indicator_text: A wide breadth of lobbying activities is included (oral, written, electronic communications; organizing meetings for others; events; phone/email contact).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute. Score 1 if the activity definition explicitly covers oral AND written AND electronic communications (all three). Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Registration changes (new registrations, deregistrations, new clients) are disclosed at least monthly.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + registration form. Original FOCAL text said 'close to real time (eg, daily)'; operationalized to 'at least monthly' as clean-cut threshold. Score 1 if changes are disclosed monthly or more frequently. Score 0 if quarterly or less frequent.
notes: Monthly threshold chosen as clean cut; the International Standards reference from Lacy-Nichols prose cites 'minimum quarterly ideally close to real-time' — monthly is between those anchors.

indicator_text: Lobbying activities (compensation/expenditure reports) are disclosed at least monthly.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + expenditure form + filing-cadence documentation. Score 1 if activity reports are filed monthly or more frequently. Score 0 if quarterly or less frequent. Record the actual cadence in notes.

indicator_text: Executive calendar / meeting-log disclosures (where such a mechanism exists) are published at least monthly.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + executive-calendar policy documentation. Original FOCAL text said 'Ministerial diaries' — renamed to 'executive calendar disclosure' for US context. Score 1 ONLY if the state has such a mechanism AND it is published at least monthly. Score 0 if no mechanism exists OR exists but is less frequent. Most US states will score 0 (no mechanism).
notes: Most US states have no equivalent mechanism; score 0 for them is informative not a defect.

indicator_text: The lobbyist register is accessible online.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: landing. Score 1 if there is a publicly accessible online register. Score 0 if registration is paper-only or the online register requires authentication for basic access.

indicator_text: Executive calendar / meeting-log disclosures are accessible online (where such a mechanism exists).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: landing + executive-calendar page. Renamed from 'diaries'. Score 1 ONLY if such disclosures exist AND are online. Score 0 if no mechanism OR exists only on paper.

indicator_text: Data is available without user registration or login for basic access.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: landing + search + bulk_download. Score 1 if any core register content is accessible without creating an account. Score 0 if account creation is required for any non-filer access.
notes: Decomposed from FOCAL's compound indicator 3.3 (5 sub-conditions). See focal_2024_indicators.csv for original verbatim text.

indicator_text: Data is available free of charge.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: landing + bulk_download + any purchasing-page captures. Score 1 if data access is free. Score 0 if any basic access tier requires payment.

indicator_text: Data is available under an open license (no re-use restrictions).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: landing + terms-of-use + data_dictionary. Score 1 if an open license is published (CC0, CC-BY, public domain, or equivalent) OR no restrictive license is imposed. Score 0 if terms of use impose redistribution or commercial-use restrictions.

indicator_text: Data is available in a non-proprietary format.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: bulk_download + data_dictionary. Score 1 if bulk data is downloadable in CSV/JSON/XML/TSV (any open format). Score 0 if only PDF or Excel-only (.xls/.xlsx) is offered.

indicator_text: Data is machine-readable.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: bulk_download. Score 1 if data is structured (not PDF-only, not image-scan, not unstructured HTML). Score 0 if the only available format is PDF, image, or HTML-only.

indicator_text: Data is downloadable in bulk (as files or a database).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: bulk_download. Score 1 if a bulk-download mechanism exists (ZIP/CSV/database dump). Score 0 if only record-by-record access via search UI.

indicator_text: Data is searchable with simultaneous sorting across multiple criteria.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: search UI. Score 1 if the portal's search supports filtering on at least 3 criteria simultaneously (e.g., lobbyist name + year + client) AND sorting of results. Score 0 otherwise. Flag if SPA-rendered and uninspectable from curl snapshot.
notes: SPA-only portals may require Playwright for honest scoring; flag rather than null-score.

indicator_text: Records use unique identifiers (for lobbyists, principals, and organizations).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: data_dictionary + bulk_download sample. Score 1 if records carry stable numeric or string IDs distinguishable from names (e.g., lobbyist_id, principal_id). Score 0 if only names are used.

indicator_text: Lobbying data is linked or interconnected with other data sets (e.g., campaign finance).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: data_dictionary + landing + any cross-referenced portal pages. Score 1 if the portal explicitly connects lobbying records to another dataset (campaign finance disclosure records; business-entity registry). Score 0 if lobbying data is siloed.

indicator_text: Historical data is archived and downloadable.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: historical_archive + bulk_download. Score 1 if data from prior years (at least 5 years back) is downloadable. Score 0 if only current-year data is accessible.

indicator_text: Changes or updates to records are flagged and tracked.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: data_dictionary + bulk_download sample. Score 1 if the portal or data model exposes update timestamps or change flags per record. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Full names of lobbyists and organizations are recorded (not abbreviations or ambiguous names).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary + sample record. Score 1 if the registration schema requires full legal names. Score 0 if initials or abbreviations are accepted.

indicator_text: Contact details (address, telephone, and/or website) are recorded for lobbyists and organizations.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if at least 2 of (address / phone / email or website) are required fields. Score 0 if fewer are required.

indicator_text: Legal form (public, private, not-for-profit, NGO, government) is recorded for registered organizations.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if legal-form is a required field. Score 0 if not collected or optional.

indicator_text: A business entity identifier (state entity ID or federal EIN) is recorded for registered organizations.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. FOCAL original said 'company registration number'; US-state translation allows either state Secretary-of-State entity ID or federal EIN. Score 1 if either is a required field.

indicator_text: Industry sector / sub-sector is recorded for registered organizations.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if a sector field (e.g., NAICS code or freeform sector text) is required. Score 0 if not collected.

indicator_text: Type of lobbyist contract (salaried in-house vs. contracted consultant) is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if contract type is a required field distinguishing employee from contractor. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Prior public-office experience (offices held and dates of service) is recorded for registered lobbyists.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary + sample record. Score 1 if the registration schema requires disclosure of prior public offices held. Score 0 if not collected or only optional.

indicator_text: The state publishes (or enforces) a database of public officials subject to a cooling-off period / lobbying ban.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: statute + ethics-commission page. Score 1 if there is a statutory cooling-off period AND a published list of officials bound by it (or an enforcement mechanism for identifying them). Score 0 if the cooling-off period exists only as statute with no enforcement/database mechanism, or if no such period exists.

indicator_text: Lobbyist-client relationships are recorded (client lists for consultant lobbyists and firms).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if lobbyist registrations must list the clients they represent. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: For associations / membership organizations, the member or sponsor list is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if association registrations must disclose their member/sponsor list. Score 0 if not required.

indicator_text: Board seats held by registered lobbyists (in associations, companies) are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if lobbyists must disclose board seats they hold. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Direct business associations between lobbyists and public officials (or candidates / household members) are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if direct financial or business relationships with officials must be disclosed. Score 0 if not required.

indicator_text: Total lobbying income (for consultant lobbyists / lobby firms) is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if total income is a required disclosure. Score 0 otherwise.
notes: Applies to consultant lobbyists and firms.

indicator_text: Lobbying income is itemized per client.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if per-client income itemization is required. Score 0 if only aggregate totals are required.

indicator_text: Income sources (including government agencies, foundations, companies) and amounts received are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if income-source categories are required disclosures. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Number of lobbyists employed or contracted is recorded (headcount and FTE equivalent).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: registration form + expenditures form. Score 1 if lobbyist counts (headcount OR FTE) are required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Amount of time (hours) spent on lobbying is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if time/hours spent lobbying is a required disclosure. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Total lobbying expenditure (in-house + consulting) is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if total expenditure is a required disclosure. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Compensated vs. uncompensated lobbying activities are distinguished in disclosures.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if the disclosure schema distinguishes paid from pro-bono lobbying. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Expenditure is itemized per issue / policy area.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if per-issue expenditure itemization is required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Expenditure on membership / sponsorship of lobbying organizations (trade associations) is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + data_dictionary. Score 1 if payments to trade associations / lobbying coalitions must be disclosed. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: Expenditures benefiting public officials (gifts, meals, travel, honoraria, in-kind support) are recorded, including the official benefited and the lobbyist's employer/principal.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + statute + data_dictionary. Score 1 if gifts/benefits to officials are required disclosures with beneficiary and sponsor named. Score 0 if such disclosures are absent or anonymized.

indicator_text: Campaign or political contributions by lobbyists (including in-kind) are recorded (or cross-linked from campaign finance disclosure).
scoring_guidance: Evidence: expenditures form + cross-reference to campaign-finance portal. Score 1 if lobbyist political contributions are disclosed OR the lobbying portal cross-references the state's campaign-finance disclosures. Score 0 if no such linkage exists.

indicator_text: (If a contact-log mechanism exists.) Organization/interest represented (beneficiary) is recorded per contact.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if the state has a contact-log mechanism AND this field is required. Score 0 if no mechanism OR field not required. Most US states score 0.
notes: Category 8 auto-scores 0 for states lacking a contact-log mechanism — FOCAL category defined at the indicator level accepts this.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Names of persons contacted and their position/role are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Institution / department contacted is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) For meetings, names of all attendees are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Date of contact is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Form of contact (in-person, video conference, phone call) is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Location of contact is recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Materials shared during contact (excluding commercially sensitive materials) are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Topics / issues discussed are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Outcomes sought (legislation/policies supported or opposed) are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.

indicator_text: (If contact-log exists.) Targeted areas of public policy or specific legislative references (bill numbers) are recorded.
scoring_guidance: Evidence: contact-log form + data_dictionary. Score 1 ONLY if mechanism exists AND field required. Score 0 otherwise.
